text.title: The PERFECT Trip to Madrid Spain! Best Things to Do & Eat (Travel Guide)
text.u: [Music]
I'm so excited I'm so
[Music]
excited oh my
gosh welcome to Madrid we are so excited
to be showing you around this city over
the next few days and showing you some
of the best things to do eat and see
speechless this thing is massive we're
going to be showing you some of the top
spots as well as a few hidden gyms here
that a lot of people don't know about oh
my God look at that I think this might
be my favorite thing that we've done in
Madrid so
far right now we are standing in plao
which is like their Time Square it is
the busiest pla in the entire city as
many as 51,000 Vehicles pass through
these intersections and it can hold
10,000 people at one time there are
Billboards everywhere it is absolutely
packed with people it is such a Vibe
it's also right along graan Vio which is
one of the busiest streets in Madrid and
it is just a short walk to the pla mayor
[Music]
no trip to Madrid would be complete
without visiting the pla mayor or the
main Square this was first opened in
1620 by King Philip III they have a
statue in the center dedicated to him
and this basa was the hub for everything
back in historic Madrid they would use
this for bulll fights fiestas executions
also concerts there's balconies
surrounding the entire Plaza that they
would watch these events from now they
are hotel rooms like give you a gorgeous
vantage point and Below you'll find tons
of cute little bars and restaurants and
shops we're not personally dining or
drinking at any of them just because it
is a very tsty area and the prices are
higher and if you just walk a few blocks
outside the plaza you can find so many
hidden gems with great prices and a much
more local
[Music]
Vibe a lot of people like to go to the
Marcato San Miguel which is right next
to the fla mayor it's a historic Market
where you can find fresh goods like
Meats cheeses tons of little toer
restaurants it's a really nice
atmosphere but it's is very touristy if
you're looking for more of an authentic
experience I highly recommend going to
some of the other markets that are
around town several of which have now
been turned into more International
indoor Food Markets there's marado and
tone Martin but today we came to vosa
which is on the north side of town our
first up for the day is Migo you can get
a lot of the traditional Spanish tapas
here they also have specialty items so
we ordered a bio with crab and
langoustine which is crawfish and a
lovely lime mayonnaise and we got s Moro
which is a cold soup made normally with
tomatoes garlic bread and olive oil this
one uses cherries and it has green apple
in it I'm very excited about it what did
you do did you make a mess oh my God
stop making a mess I can't not make a
mess you know who I
am it's like a monster of a sandwich I'm
not quite sure if I should just eat it
or cut it I'm just going to eat
it I have radish in there some lettuce o
really good there's a lot of traditional
things you should try when coming to
Spain sangria and paa I think are the
first thing that comes to everybody's
mind sangria is delicious and you can
definitely order it here but vermouth is
actually their national drink so I
highly recommend trying either white or
red verou it kind of has like a little
bit of an apptivo Anis flavor to it gets
your digestive juices flowing and it's
really really refreshing and delicious
smells like a Christmas Day I feel smell
like Christmas one food from Spain that
I absolutely love is Moria which is
blood sausage it's kind of an
off-putting thing to hear for people who
are not familiar with it but I promise
it is so tasty they add rice and spices
and put it in a casing this one does not
disappoint in any way shape or
form speechless this thing is mass we
love Spanish food but this is week seven
for us being in this country and this is
a really great internal Market with a
lot of food to offer other than just
traditional Spanish TAA we came to a
pastrami spot we got the spicy Sano
pastrami these are really high quality
brisket for this and they brined
themselves for 5 weeks look at
that
[Music]
oh this morning we are headed to the
Royal Palace with a 2hour guided tour
that we booked with G guide who's the
sponsor of this video G guide is a
online booking service that offers
offers thousands of different
experiences and tours in cities across
the world anytime we visit a new city we
always make sure to check get your guide
they offer everything from cooking
classes and Food Tours sightseeing and
history and we've used them in Barcelona
and now in Madrid and we always love our
experiences we're going to have links
for all of the activities including this
exact tour in the video description
below so when you're visiting Madrid you
can make the most of your time in this
beautiful city I think the tour is
starting
[Music]
I'm so excited I'm so excited we've been
on a Bridgerton kick so I'm like very
time period oriented with this and I
feel like I'm living a little bit of my
Bridgerton life we've learned that the
palace is the largest in Eastern Europe
it is double the size of Vera it's
almost double the size of fuckingham
Palace it doesn't look that grand from
the outside well I mean it does look
very Grand from the
outside but it's deceiving because
there's four levels below the ground
which is why it is one of the largest
palaces they used to have 6,000 people
working here in order to make this
Palace be a reality the king and queen
actually don't live here today they have
a separate residence they do come back
here for formal events but they stopped
living here in 1931 we're only going to
be able to film in the two First rooms
and we're going to be visiting 25 other
rooms on this tour which all to take
pictures or videos
[Music]
there Spain's Royal Palace was built
under orders from King Philip I in 1738
was built on top of the former site of a
9th century Islamic Fortress the
building was built with Baroque and
neoclassical designs by three Italian
Architects it took 18 years to complete
and when finished occupied over 1.4
million square ft no expense was spared
on the palace's design nearly every room
had hand painted Fresco on the ceilings
gilded in gold and massive
chandeliers over its 260e history The
Palace has undergone only minor cosmetic
changes very much looks as it would have
under King Philip the F's
rule that tour was absolutely fantastic
we were only able to show you a fraction
of what we even saw I don't even think
we saw a full 1% of the 3500 rooms here
if you thought what we showed you was
opulent let me just tell you the other
rooms that we couldn't show you were so
much more Grand and ornate and
incredible the amount of money and work
and care that went into crafting these
rooms is
unreal you definitely have to come see
this for yourself these tickets sell out
really fast this is something you want
to book before you arrive and I
definitely recommend the 9:45 tour it's
the earliest tour they open at 10:00
a.m. you can also upgrade to do a
kitchen tour because the kitchen was a
completely separate area downside that's
only offered in Spanish three times a
day don't forget we'll have a link down
below for you to book this tour yourself
[Music]
today we came to one of the historic
restaurants in Madrid ptin is known for
being the oldest restaurant in the world
in the Guinness books of World Records
it's been open for over 300 years they
specialize in coacho which is a
beastling pig that is slow roasted it
comes from the region of sovia we've
tried it there at one of the famous
restaurants and it is absolutely
incredible we weren't able to get a
reservation there so you do want to book
early if you'd like to dine there
instead we came to P de laa to try seed
do and it is one of my favorite things
to eat in all of Spain but I don't think
a lot of people know about it it's a
traditional stew and all the ingredients
like chicken Teo a bunch of different
type of pork and they put potatoes and
garbanza they just put it in this old
clay pot and cook it over coals for 5
hours this is a really cool building it
was built in 1642 it was the Old Mill
for this area and it went abandoned for
several years until a developer put
millions of dollars rebuilding it and
turning it into this restaurant and
they've done such a beautiful job it
feels like you're stepping into a
restaurant from 1642 if you also want to
try lch they are known for that at this
restaurant it is a slow roasted lamb
which is a specialty of this region as
well we've had laasa before and it is
magnific but letho is going to be a
belly Buster it is a very heavy plate
it's the soup or the sofa is the first
course it comes with little flat noodles
called Fidos this is my favorite part of
the soup honestly I could just have the
broth oh you just get a lot of fat in
the mouth feel but it's so light and
refreshing at the same time we're going
to have so much leftovers this this
whole pot is just the first course of
this and it's at least half full this is
not something you'd want to come to
order in the summertime though it is a
very warming meal this is typically
found in winter time this is enough to
feed four people this plate is massive
we have cabbage chicken chiso Moria
blood sausage potato carrot actual bones
with the bone marrow and then we have
some pieces of really juicy fat bacon
with gbono you can mix it with the soup
if you'd like or you can just eat it
separately everyone has their own little
method of eating cido I don't even know
where to begin the garbanzo are I think
the best we've had in cido they're
really flavorful we didn't even put a
dent in it it's ridiculous I'm so full
got doggy bag though yes we taken this
home it's going to be like lunch for 2
days going to have a topito and then
siesta
2 hours later Madrid has a Holy Trinity
of museums where you can find three
fantastic art museums that are
world-renowned we're going to be going
to two of them today starting at the
prao Museum we just arrived and the line
to buy tickets is literally around the
building it is massive but because we
use get your guide we get to skip the
line and go straight in and I'm pretty
sure the tickets are like the same price
if not like a Euro difference well worth
it we can't film here unfortunately we
will be able to show you some of the
artwork from the next Museum we're going
to this one is known for older pieces
from the 13th 14th 15th 16th century and
they have collections from all across
the world this museum is massive we
spent about 3 hours in there with an
audio guide and we only saw one floor I
would plan to spend all day or much
longer than 3 hours cuz we only scratch
the surface the next Museum we're going
to be going to has a lot more modern
contemporary artists you'll find things
like Dolly Goya Picasso which we really
enjoyed
[Music]
nothing quite kicks off a morning in
Madrid like chos Chate this sweet treat
is very famous for breakfast it's deep
fried dough that you dip in this thick
sweet Rich beautiful chocolate it is
heaven we came to an institution which
is Sanz this has been here since
1894 and is by far the most famous spot
for getting chos quate normally you'll
find a line that goes around the block
so be prepared to wait but luckily we
came around 11:00 a.m. on a Tuesday and
it's not too busy we were able to stag a
spot
it's so delicious oh my gosh I might
like this one more you get more
chocolate on the surface
area I would suggest if you're not a
chocolate fan skip this
dish but if you like a sweet treat
[Music]
the city of Madrid dates back to 865 ad
so there's a lot of history here but
most of the buildings that you're going
to see date back to the 17th and 18th
century we're walking right now on Cay
Alcala which is one of the most historic
streets it's also where you find a lot
of the important monuments for Madrid
like the bank of Spain and the thelis
fountain if you haven't watched CAD
papel it's a fantastic show that is
based here in Madrid where they break
into the bank of Spain and the Min there
really is a gold vault below the bank of
Spain about 37 ft below the fountain
which holds like billions of dollars in
gold we're going to get out of this
crazy intersection and head to rtio Park
which is like the Central Park of Madrid
[Music]
there is a life IAD in this
city hurry to cut my tea I can take what
I need to get
by doesn't make it
easy being in R Park is so refreshing we
spent now 2 weeks in the city and Madrid
and Barcelona are stunning but they are
completely cement cities which is
massive buildings and you just don't
really get much nature and this is over
300 acres of lush trees beautiful
manicured Gardens and fountains it is a
true Oasis in the city rer park was
actually built by King Philip IV in 1640
this was his private Garden they had his
Duke purchased a land which at the time
that it was purchased made up about half
the size of the city of Madrid and then
in 1838 Queen Isabelle decided to make
it open to the public opening the nine
gates in the park and you'll just find
people from all walks of life tourists
local walking their dogs people having a
nice little picnic so people doing yoga
in the park it is absolutely beautiful I
think this might be my favorite thing
that we've done in Madrid so far if
you're looking for a really romantic
like Day date you could easily pack a
little picnic grab some homone and
cheeses and a bottle of wine come have a
little lunch here or a snack here and
then go for a rowboat ride on the
lake the rose gardens here alone are
just breathtaking can you imagine
imagine just getting to strolled through
this as your private residence have been
a king or a queen during those days is
insane The Gardens have changed a lot
this whole area has changed a lot over
the years it used to be um like
interconnected waterways and Canal
systems mostly and there were some areas
of greenery in between the king even had
gondolas shipped in from Italy so he
could Traverse the waterways they would
have plays here what a grand time only
for the wealthy so really not that grand
of a Time
[Music]
out of 10 experience this park is
absolutely lovely such a hidden Oasis to
get you away from the hustle and bustle
of the city we're now heading all the
way across town so that we can get to an
iconic spot for the sunset but it's
going to be a long haul to get there
there is a Metro System in Madrid but
it's not the most well connected
especially compared to like Barcelona
you can also take the bus you can rent
bikes there's like E Scooters that you
can rent as well and of course you can
take a taxi or Uber we're going to take
the metro but it will include like a
40-minute journey to get there at least
we're walking through the park I'm not
mad about that it's absolutely
beautiful we made it to prin Pio which
is the highest point Madrid giving you
absolutely stunning vistas of the city
and of course one of the reasons it is
such a popular spot for sunset there's a
beautiful Park here there also a really
cool Egyptian temple called the Temple
of debad this was built in Egypt in the
2 century and it was gifted to Madrid in
Egypt where it was located they were
building a dam and it was going to be
flooded so it was a donation it was
rebuilt Stone by Stone here in the city
center and opened in 1976 to the public
it faced East and West just as it would
have if it was in Egypt I'm sure that
has something to do with the original
purpose of the temple oh and another fun
fact this park has a lot of history to
it this is where the 1808 battle took
place against Napoleon there was like
Mass executions when Napoleon's troops
came in against the rebels this is also
where the uprising happened that started
the Spanish Civil War in 1936 once again
I am impressed with the parks of Madrid
for sure this is a lovely Green Space
there's a lot of people just reading
just chilling out after work I'm really
looking forward to the sense that I
think it's going to be epic
[Music]
un now it's time for some tapas and some
drinks so Tapas you're supposed to move
from place to place it's actually a verb
tapad which is to go from place to place
eating small snacks and having a drink
with each one you can absolutely go and
just sit down and order a bunch of Tapas
at one place but it's really not how the
locals consume Tapas you're supposed to
move from bar to bar eating Tapas and
having a drink at each place and asking
what they specialty is there's tons of
like local traditional Tapas that you
can order a lot of the restaurants will
put like a spin on it for example this
place we order cettas which normally
comes with bamal hamon inside and they
deep fry it these however are made with
blue cheese and sundried tomato we've
never seen that before so we had to
order them this place is known for their
Tortilla de batas but they only serve it
in the morning to early afternoon so
unfortunately we are not going to taste
test that it's one of our favorites eat
M
funky our second stop on our tapa crawl
is down at the street papava Baja
there's so many different tapas
restaurants that you can come to making
it a really popular place to do a tapa
crawl and most the restaurants on the
street are absolutely fantastic we came
to Kasa luucas we've been here many
times before they're known for their
croquettas but today we got their other
specialty which is the rabo de tto in
other words the bll tail sounds like a
really odd piece of meat to eat but is
actually quite tender and delicious and
it is a byproduct of bull fighting they
put it on a puree of potatoes with some
olive oil and crushed up
pistachios just as good as I remembered
our last stop is Lucio they have two
sides actually we were recommended to
this spot from our friends Carmen and
Foo they specialize
inos which is french fries with two
beautifully fried eggs on top that you
end up cutting or breaking which is Theo
and then you just cut it in all of the
juice from the weos like kind of sofs
into the french fries it's Heavenly they
normally have this for dinner this is a
very typical dinner dish if you were
just in a traditional Spanish home and
we ended up adding chista which is a
special type of sausage from bass
country it looks like little chitty so
but it's
not it's so
simple which I think is the beauty of it
it's our last day in Madrid so it's our
last chance to enjoy Spanish food I'm
not going to lie I'm not ready for it we
came to eloo De laara it's a cute little
restaurant just a 3-minute walk from the
pla mayor which is I think somewhat of a
Hidden Gem among tourists this came as a
recommendation from our Uber driver who
has not seared us wrong so far I'm
excited it's like perfectly
tender has just right spice level we
ordered kopo which is an asuan dish
which is a region in Spain
they take two very thinly pounded beel
slices and then they stuff it with
cheese and ham so it's almost like a
schnitzel and a cordon blue but Spanish
style and then they bread it and they
fry it it is absolutely fantastic and
this place is known for it oh my God
look at
that wow we end up having someone
watching us as we were talking on
YouTube who was like I just want to let
you know this truly is my favorite place
I'm from Madrid this is one of my
favorite places to visit she did
recommend trying the steak or any of the
marisco dishes also if they have pulo or
octopus for the day you should ask for
that so don't just go for the kopo
although it is fantastic and if you've
never tried it it's a great place to do
so but they have a lot more to offer
here and the owner Miguel here is the
sweetest was just so friendly and
brought us some more desserts so these
are two uh desserts from Galia both are
made from almond or almonds it's called
deas which is like the roof tiles they
use here in Spain cuz it kind of
resembles that and the other one is
called the T de
Santiago oh
[Music]
good but now we're off to
eat san fran in the House San Fran in
the house bro the haa Sophia or not ha
Sophia that's the it's simple oh my
God
okay I didn't understand that part we're
just going to act like
